name: Slab & Buddy Allocation
def: Allocator designs: slabs serve fixed-size objects from caches; buddy splits/merges power-of-two blocks.
problem: Allocate efficiently with low fragmentation for kernel/system workloads.
mechanism: Slab pre-allocates caches of same-size objects (fast reuse, cache-warm); buddy maintains free lists of power-of-two blocks, splitting and coalescing on demand.
benefits: Slab: O(1) typed alloc, low fragmentation, cache warmth
Buddy: fast split/coalesce; bounded external fragmentation
limits: Buddy: internal fragmentation (rounding up to powers of two)
failures: Buddy rounding wasting up to ~2× memory
Slab cache bloat for many sizes
whenNot: Application code where the general allocator suffices
myths: "One allocator fits all" — kernels use specialised allocators per workload
